channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italy's Meloni hopes to attend Trump inauguration as she downplays his Greenland and Panama comments
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday she didn’t believe President-elect Donald Trump actually intends to use military force to seize control of Greenland or the Panama Canal, saying she read his comments more as a warning to China and other global players to keep their hands off
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greenland important for national security: Waltz
item.description: Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.), President-elect Trump’s pick for national security adviser, said Wednesday that Greenland is important for national security, leaning into Trump's expressed interest in acquiring the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique opposition leader returns from self-imposed exile as police fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's main opposition leader returned from self-imposed exile on Thursday clutching a bible and saying he still rejects the results of a disputed election last year that has sparked more than two months of protests and a violent crackdown by security forces.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's Ishiba heads to Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba on Thursday began a trip to Malaysia and Indonesia as part of an effort to strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as China's threats grow in the region.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Days before Venezuela’s presidential inauguration, self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González was in Panama Wednesday rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists say they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice that's at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: Paolo Santalucia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks was freed Wednesday and returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon 'not aware of any plans' to take Greenland by force
item.description: The Pentagon on Wednesday said it was “not aware” of any plans to invade Greenland if ordered to do so, after President-elect Trump a day earlier refused to rule out using military force to seize Denmark's autonomous territory. “I'm certainly not going to get into hypothetical situations.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany warns Trump after Greenland takeover threat
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz sent a warning to President-elect Trump over border rules as the incoming president continues his threat of taking over Greenland. Scholz said Wednesday that he spoke with several European leaders and the president of the European Council.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Jeffries: 'Confused' by Trump's 'obsession' with Greenland
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) hammered President-elect Trump on Wednesday for what he characterized as an “obsession” with the idea of the U.S. taking over Greenland, accusing Trump of fixating on an issue of no consequence for working-class Americans.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: France says EU won't tolerate attack on borders after Trump Greenland takeover remarks
item.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot says the European Union (EU) will stand firm in its alliance in the face of repeated comments from President-elect Trump about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli Army says it recovered body of hostage in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli Army on Wednesday said it had recovered the body from the Gaza Strip of an Israeli citizen who was kidnapped by Hamas during its terrorist attack on Oct. 7 and held hostage, and was working to identify the remains of another body as a possible Israeli hostage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: 23-year-old hiker found after surviving for 2 weeks in Australian mountain range
item.description: A medical student from Melbourne who was missing in a remote Australian mountain range for two weeks survived on two muesli bars, foraged berries and creek water, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of a quake that killed 126 in freezing, high-altitude Tibet
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Relief teams in western China shifted their focus to resettling survivors after a search Wednesday for any remaining victims of a deadly earthquake that struck a day earlier near a holy city for Tibetan Buddhists.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Controversial founder of French far right, Jean-Marie Le Pen, dies aged 96
item.description: Jean-Marie Le Pen, who founded France's far-right National Front party, died at 96 years old. Le Pen was a controversial and confrontational figure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
